name: Update catalog item

requestModel.id: int [Range(1,10000)] — identifies the item to update (in request body, not path)
requestModel.name: string [Required]
requestModel.pictureUri: string (accepted but ignored — PictureUri is not updated by this endpoint)

targetRestContract.notes: The item ID is embedded in the request body, not the URL path — the legacy route is PUT api/catalog-items with no path parameter. The Java target should use PUT /api/catalog-items/{id} for RESTful conformance. PictureUri is NOT updated by this endpoint regardless of what the request body contains.

notes: The legacy route embeds the item ID in the request body (not the path). The Java target should move it to a path parameter: PUT /api/catalog-items/{id}.

PictureUri, PictureBase64, and PictureName are accepted in the request but completely ignored — UpdateDetails only updates Name, Description, and Price; UpdateBrand and UpdateType handle their respective fields.

DataAnnotations on UpdateCatalogItemRequest ([Range], [Required]) are metadata only — MinimalApi does not auto-validate them. The Guard.Against clauses inside the entity mutation methods are the actual runtime validation. If they throw, ExceptionMiddleware returns HTTP 500.
No AutoMapper — CatalogItemDto is manually constructed field-by-field inside HandleAsync.
Requires JWT Bearer token with ADMINISTRATORS role (BlazorShared.Authorization.Constants.Roles.ADMINISTRATORS = 'Administrators').
